affordances.name: Signal preserving compression
affordances.mechanism: When useful truth is not landing because of information shape, require the answer to prune, order, and package the message so the core signal survives without stripping out the decision-relevant complexity.

use_when: Complexity is real but attention is scarce.
The same decision is failing because of message shape, not message truth.
The communicator must choose what not to say while preserving action-critical signal.
do_not_use_when: Compression would erase system interconnections or weak signals that matter.
The message needs more evidence, not cleaner packaging.
Emotional, stakeholder, or implementation context is being stripped away as noise.
case_evidence_needed: The core signal that must survive.
The material to prune or sequence.
The complexity that cannot be safely compressed.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to make the message easier to process while naming what the simplified map leaves out.
good_output_shape: Warns where compression would distort the territory.

diagnostic_questions: What is the smallest message that still preserves the decision signal?
What important interconnection would be lost if we compressed further?
Is the failure truth quality or message shape?
misuse_guards: Do not confuse information volume with signal.
Do not compress away meaningful weak signals.
Do not turn a map into the territory.

source_evidence.source_quote: Best when the communicator must choose what not to say

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 13 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports information theory as signal-preserving compression and sequencing.
dropped_material: Did not treat compression as always good.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether this card improves compactness without flattening the case.

absence_records.reason: The source warns against boiling the ocean and gathering data for its own sake rather than preserving actionable signal.
The source says adoption can fail because sequencing, chunking, framing, or contrast prevents comprehension, so signal must be shaped for its receiver.

source_evidence.source_quote: sequencing, chunking, framing, or contrast is preventing comprehension and adoption
minimize the **cognitive expenditure** required for the audience

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 13 source reading found strong reviewed depth for information theory as signal-preserving packaging under cognitive constraints.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet review should check whether this card helps reason about packet burden itself.
